macro.title: Macro Economics
macro.desc: US Treasury Yield, NASDAQ, Gold, High Yield Spread

onchain.title: On-chain Data
onchain.desc: BTC Hashrate, Mining Difficulty, Stablecoin Market Cap

tech.title: Technical Indicators
tech.desc: 200-Week Moving Average, Miner Breakeven, ATH Drawdown

cryptoIndex.title: Crypto Market Cap Index
cryptoIndex.subtitle: A fixed-basket, market-cap weighted index built from the current top-N assets to approximate the core crypto market.
cryptoIndex.tableSubtitle: The leaderboard uses current rankings, and the index replays the same basket through historical market caps.

backtest.centerSubtitle: Use this page to choose a strategy, tune inputs, and start a backtest or paper run. History stays on the right, while full analysis moves into a dedicated detail page.
backtest.centerHint: The workspace now focuses on setup and record picking
backtest.editorSubtitle: This page is dedicated to copying an existing version, building a new one from scratch, and managing indicators or templates. When you're done, go back to the backtest center to run it.
backtest.editorHint: The editor now focuses on strategy design instead of mixed execution and analysis
backtest.editorSeedSubtitle: Pick an existing strategy version as the base, or start from blank. The editor on the right stays focused on that seed only.
backtest.detailSubtitle: This page is dedicated to reviewing one run: returns, drawdown, trades, and charts. The left history rail only helps you switch runs and compare them.
backtest.detailEmptyTitle: This run could not be found
backtest.detailEmptySubtitle: The record may no longer exist, or you opened an outdated link.
backtest.executionConfigHint: Spot only runs long logic. Short branches become active only when the strategy is switched to futures.
backtest.trendBranchHint: Decide whether the market is trending first, then run only the trend-following logic.
backtest.rangeBranchHint: Only when trend is inactive should the editor route into the range buy-low, exit-high logic.
backtest.versionDraftRequired: Strategy key and version name are required
backtest.templateDraftRequired: Template key and template name are required
backtest.trailingOffsetOnly: Enable only after offset is reached
backtest.historyBrowseHint: Pick a backtest or paper run here to open its detail view
backtest.selectForCompareHint: Select runs from the history panel to compare
backtest.noCompareSelection: No historical runs selected for comparison
backtest.versionCompareHint: Compare the latest run of each selected version in the current strategy
backtest.noVersionCompareData: No comparable version runs for the current strategy
backtest.rangeRequired: Choose a complete backtest date range first
backtest.paperRecentHint: Continuous paper sessions and stopped paper records appear here.
backtest.confirmDeleteRun: Delete this backtest record? This cannot be undone.
backtest.confirmDeletePaperRun: Delete this paper run? If it is still running, it will be stopped first.
backtest.backtestHint: Backtest computes the whole historical research result in one pass.
backtest.paperHint: Paper mode advances positions and equity only when a new candle closes.
backtest.paperUnsupportedHint: This strategy does not support paper mode yet.
backtest.readonlyStrategyHint: This built-in strategy is read-only and cannot be copied into the editor.
backtest.scriptedReadonlyHint: This built-in scripted strategy is backtest-only for now.

factorResearch.title: Factor Research
factorResearch.subtitle: Bring price targets, macro, on-chain, sentiment, and derived price factors into one research sheet, then test correlation, lag, stability, and quantile spreads together.
factorResearch.hint: Filter factors first, decide on strategy work later
factorResearch.recentRunsHint: Recent research outputs stay here so you can jump back to an earlier sample quickly.
factorResearch.categoryScopeHint: Narrow down broad categories first, then decide whether to hand-pick individual factors.
factorResearch.factorPoolHint: If you do not pick individual factors, the page will use every factor under the selected categories.
factorResearch.rankingSubtitle: Scores combine correlation, lead profile, stability, and quantile spread.
factorResearch.noRanking: No factor ranking is available right now
factorResearch.blendSubtitle: Remove highly duplicated or weak factors first, then keep a smaller and cleaner blended input set.
factorResearch.noBlend: This research run did not produce a usable blend
factorResearch.executionSubtitle: Send the current research result straight into backtest or paper mode without copying settings manually.
factorResearch.executionHint: Entry and exit thresholds default to the suggested blend values, but you can tighten or loosen them here before running.
factorResearch.forwardMetricsSubtitle: Review whether the same relationship still holds across 1, 3, 5, and 10 forward bars.
factorResearch.seriesSubtitle: Standardize both series and compare whether they move on a similar rhythm.
factorResearch.rollingSubtitle: Do not trust one average number. Check whether the relationship keeps showing up over time.
factorResearch.lagSubtitle: See at which lead bar the factor has the clearest relationship with future returns.
factorResearch.quantileSubtitle: Split factor values into groups and compare future returns across buckets.
factorResearch.blendSeriesSubtitle: Overlay the composite factor score and price to see whether the blend tracks the market rhythm more cleanly.
factorResearch.noDescription: This factor does not have an extra description yet.

portfolioBalance.title: Portfolio Balance
portfolioBalance.subtitle: Track the portfolio with live prices and directly see how much each asset should be increased or reduced.
portfolioBalance.strategySubtitle: Scheduled review + drift bands + minimum trade size
portfolioBalance.configSubtitle: Portfolios are saved as long-lived plans. Target weights do not need to add up to exactly 100 because the planner normalizes them.
portfolioBalance.importSource: Current holdings were imported from paper run #{id}, last updated at {updated}
portfolioBalance.triggerSubtitle: When an asset falls below the lower band or rises above the upper band, it should be considered for the next rebalance.
portfolioBalance.noteNormalized: Your input weights add up to {sum}%, so the planner normalized them automatically.
portfolioBalance.noteEqualWeight: No target weights are filled yet, so the planner is using an equal-weight mix for now.

reasons.on_track: The portfolio is still inside the tolerance band and the scheduled review date has not arrived yet.
reasons.scheduled_review: The scheduled review date has arrived, so the portfolio should be checked and adjusted under the policy.
reasons.band_breach: A weight has breached the band, but the resulting trade is still too small after the minimum trade filter.
reasons.cashflow_first: The target portfolio value is above the current value, so the gap can be filled without first selling existing holdings.
reasons.full_rebalance: Closing the gap alone is not enough, so a full rebalance is warranted.
reasons.below_threshold: A review condition was met, but the minimum trade filter says the portfolio is still too small to act on.
